claimText: Transition Bonds are classified as a sub-category of green debt securities under SEBI regulations.
evidenceExcerpt: Transition Bonds are a sub-category of green debt securities as per SEBI regulations.
examUse: Statement-based question on SEBI's classification of green debt instruments.
distractorHooks: Regulated by RBI instead of SEBI
Classified as conventional corporate bonds rather than green debt

claimText: Transition Bonds are designed to help hard-to-abate sectors, such as steel and cement, raise capital for progressive decarbonization.
evidenceExcerpt: Transition Bonds help hard-to-abate sectors raise capital for progressive decarbonization.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the specific use-case of Transition Bonds.
distractorHooks: Used exclusively by renewable energy companies
Aimed at financing sovereign fiscal deficits

claimText: Additional Tier 1 (AT1) Bonds are perpetual bonds issued by banks that do not have a maturity date.
evidenceExcerpt: Additional Tier 1 (AT1) Bonds are perpetual bonds issued by banks without a maturity date.
examUse: Statement-based question on the characteristics of AT1 bonds.
distractorHooks: Have a fixed maturity period of 5 to 10 years
Issued exclusively by the Reserve Bank of India

claimText: AT1 Bonds include a call option and are issued by banks specifically to meet capital adequacy requirements.
evidenceExcerpt: AT1 bonds include a call option and are issued to meet capital adequacy requirements.
examUse: Statement-based question on banking capital norms and bond features.
distractorHooks: Include a put option allowing investors to demand early repayment
Issued to meet Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) requirements
